page: Amazon Web Services c8id.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server instance powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 96 physical cores and 192 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. It is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. The processor architecture includes a 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated vector and matrix operations.

For high-speed local data access, the instance includes 11,400 GB of bundled NVMe SSD local instance storage. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 75 Gbps. The combination of high-capacity local solid-state storage and high-throughput networking supports data-intensive operations without external storage bottlenecks.

Benchmark results place the c8id.metal-48xl in the top tier for multi-core CPU performance, including stress-ng multi-core and PassMark CPU tests. Memory bandwidth is also highly rated, achieving top-tier performance in both small-block cached and large-block uncached read/write operations. The instance demonstrates strong capabilities in specialized workloads, scoring in the top tier for software compilation, ray tracing, and database operations, alongside high throughput in Redis SET operations and static web serving. Additionally, it achieves top-tier and strong performance in LLM inference prompt processing and text generation across small, medium, and large models.

Given its bare-metal architecture, high core count, and integrated NVMe storage, the c8id.metal-48xl offers high resource density for compute-heavy and storage-intensive workloads. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio represents a tradeoff that prioritizes raw processing power over massive memory capacity. This profile makes the instance highly suitable for high-performance computing, large-scale software compilation, database hosting, real-time analytics, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: The Amazon Web Services c8id.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server designed for high-throughput workloads. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 96 physical cores and 192 vCPUs running at 3.9 GHz, paired with 384.0 GB of memory and a substantial 11,400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, the processor supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and AMX. With a 75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, this instance delivers top-tier performance in multi-core processing, memory bandwidth, and database operations. Benchmark data highlights its exceptional capabilities in software compilation, Redis operations, and LLM inference. The c8id.metal-48xl is highly suited for compute-intensive applications, database hosting, and high-performance computing tasks.
